mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls signal expanding labor income and household consumption, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positive in more than 50% of months where PAYEMS increases month-over-month for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1%, small upward adjustments validate recovery expectations rather than signal restrictive tightening, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously declining unemployment and rising industrial production indicate a non-inflationary expansion where output gaps close without immediate monetary restraint, generating positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint z-scores of UNRATE decline and INDPRO increase do not predict positive S&P 500 20-day returns with Rank IC greater than 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion defined by unemployment below 6%, month-over-month declines in industrial production predict negative 20-day S&P 500 returns as margin compression and demand saturation trigger earnings downgrades.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month declines do not coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE is below 6%.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month acceleration in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate revenue growth, leading to positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are not significantly positive with t-test p-value below 0.05 during months when INDPRO rises for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in retail sales triggers risk-off sentiment and earnings revision downgrades, causing negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when RSAFS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with a declining federal funds rate generate positive 60-day S&P 500 returns because mortgage affordability improves and residential investment accelerates without offsetting monetary tightening.
failure_condition: Joint increases in HOUST and decreases in FEDFUNDS fail to produce positive S&P 500 60-day returns in at least 60% of coincident occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP above 2% annualized confirms macro momentum that reduces recession probability, producing positive 20-day S&P 500 returns as risk premiums compress.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 45% of quarters when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% annualized.
